questions.prompt: What JavaScript runtime do Cloudflare Workers use?

choices.text: V8 isolates (the same engine Chrome uses for JS). Not Node — `fs`, native bindings, and many npm packages that rely on Node internals don't work. Web-standard APIs (fetch, Request/Response, crypto, URL) do. WASM is supported. Sub-5ms cold starts.

questions.explanation: V8 isolates give Workers fast cold starts (<5ms) and a web-standard API surface. Migrating Node code can require adjustments — particularly anywhere `fs`, native modules, or process-level Node features were used. Modern frameworks (Hono, Next.js Edge Runtime) target this environment.

questions.prompt: You want to validate JWT tokens before requests reach your origin, rejecting invalid ones at the edge. Right tool?

choices.text: A Cloudflare Worker bound to your route. Validates tokens at the edge; returns 401 for invalid; passes valid requests through to origin. Origin stops wasting CPU on bad requests; rejected requests have near-zero latency. Canonical use case for Workers.

questions.explanation: Edge auth checks are one of the highest-leverage Worker patterns. The Worker runs at every PoP, validates the token, and either rejects or forwards. Origin only sees authenticated traffic; rejected traffic never burns origin resources.
